tools.whatItDoes: Structural code bookmarking system using tree-sitter to capture semantic structure rather than fragile file:line coordinates. Semantic search via local vector embeddings (sqlite-vec, no API key): codemark reindex then codemark search --semantic "how are tokens validated?". Collections group bookmarks into task-specific sets. Git-integrated: tracks bookmarks across commits/branches. Integrations: Television (TUI fuzzy-finder), Neovim (gutter signs, visual-selection bookmarking, Telescope), Claude Code (dedicated plugin, badged "agent-ready"). 8 languages via tree-sitter: Rust, Swift, TypeScript/TSX, Python, Go, Java, Dart, C#. sources.quote: Drop: articles (a/an/the), filler (just/really/basically/actually/simply), pleasantries (sure/certainly/of course/happy to), hedging. Fragments OK. Short synonyms (big not extensive, fix not "implement a solution for"). No tool-call narration, no decorative tables/emoji, no dumping long raw error logs unless asked — quote shortest decisive line. Standard well-known tech acronyms OK (DB/API/HTTP); never invent new abbreviations (cfg/impl/req/res/fn) — tokenizer split them same as full word: zero token saved, reader still decode. Full word cheaper AND clearer. No causal arrows (→) either — own token, save nothing. Technical terms exact. Code blocks unchanged. Errors quoted exact.

| **lite** | No filler/hedging. Keep articles + full sentences. Professional but tight |
| **full** | Drop articles, fragments OK, short synonyms. Classic caveman. No tool-call narration, no decorative tables/emoji, no long raw error-log dumps unless asked. Standard acronyms OK; no invented abbreviations |
| **ultra** | Strip conjunctions when cause-then-effect stay unambiguous. One word when one word enough. State each fact once. NO prose abbreviations (cfg/impl/req/res/fn/auth), NO arrows (X → Y) — measured zero token saving under tokenizer, cost decode clarity. Code symbols, function names, API names, error strings: never touch |

Cross-project semantic search with a per-project private opt-out is a genuinely useful default for anyone running curion across multiple repos on one machine. Contrast with mem (karmugilen/mem): both minimal, but mem is zero-LLM/zero-cost while curion needs an API key for its normalization layer in exchange for safety filtering and semantic recall.

tools.whatItDoes: Skill/plugin enforcing a YAGNI decision ladder before the agent writes any code: (1) does this need to exist - skip if not, (2) does stdlib do it, (3) native platform feature, (4) installed dependency, (5) one line, (6) only then the minimum that works. Never cuts validation, error handling, security, or accessibility. Commands: /ponytail [lite|full|ultra|off], /ponytail-review (diff over-engineering check), /ponytail-audit (whole-repo audit), /ponytail-debt (harvest deferred shortcuts into a ledger), /ponytail-gain (benchmark scoreboard). 14 platforms: Claude Code, Codex, Copilot CLI, Pi, OpenCode, Gemini CLI, Antigravity, CodeWhale, OpenClaw, plus instruction-only Cursor/Windsurf/Cline/Kiro. Agentic benchmark (real Claude Code session, real FastAPI+React repo, 12 tasks, n=4): -54% LOC, -22% tokens, -20% cost, -27% time vs no-skill baseline, 100% safe. v4.7.0, 9 releases, created 12 Jun 2026.

tools.whatItDoes: Local proxy + CLI sitting between Claude Code and any LLM provider. cf wrap claude starts proxy, indexes repo (Tree-sitter AST: JS/TS/TSX/Python/Go/Rust/Java), launches Claude Code through it. Protocol translation lets Claude Code drive Ollama, OpenAI, Groq, Gemini - not just Anthropic. Repository Graph: find_symbol, analyze_impact, find_route (SQLite + HNSW vector index). Context Optimizer: tool-schema minimization, keep-newest dedup (SimHash near-dup detection), age-gated AST skeletonization, junk vaulting (lockfiles, minified bundles, base64 blobs). Surgical AST-aware patching returns unified diff (no re-read to verify). Per-workspace persistent session memory (HNSW + SQLite). Live dashboard at localhost:3000. cf doctor: 8-point install diagnosis with --fix. Prebuilt native binaries for 5 platforms. v1.0.0 released 4 Jul 2026, 23 commits.

tools.decisionRule: Only tool in this comparison making Claude Code a first-class local-model client - cf wrap claude lets you keep the Claude Code UX while routing to a fully local Ollama model, zero Anthropic API key needed. Benchmark is a single honest case study (66% fewer round-trips, 72.8% fewer input tokens vs passthrough), not yet a full suite - SWE-bench runs explicitly listed as in-development in the roadmap. Disclosed known issue: token savings can be over-reported on failed upstream requests (e.g. bad API key) since compression is logged regardless of whether the request succeeded. Claude Code fully supported; Codex/Gemini CLI experimental. Revisit once community adoption and the promised benchmark suite land.
